【Trump Signals Possible Iran Meeting; Netanyahu Confirms 10-Day Lebanon Ceasefire】President Donald Trump said a meeting with Iran could take place over the weekend, adding that both sides have “a lot of agreement” and that Tehran has “agreed to almost everything,” including commitments on nuclear restrictions. He said failure to reach a deal could see fighting resume, but expressed confidence an agreement is likely, which he said would lower oil prices and inflation. Trump also indicated he may extend the ceasefire if needed.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed a temporary 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon, stating Israel’s key demand remains the dismantling of Hezbollah and that Israeli forces will maintain an extensive security zone. Oil prices pared earlier gains following the remarks.

【US Oil Industry Urges Trump to Reject Iran Hormuz Toll Proposal】US oil executives are lobbying Donald Trump to oppose any deal allowing Iran to impose tolls on the Strait of Hormuz, warning it would set a dangerous precedent for global shipping. Industry leaders and the American Petroleum Institute say reopening the waterway, which handles about a fifth of global oil and LNG flows, is critical. They argue Iranian control would disrupt markets, raise costs and embolden other states, while some call for joint international action to secure the route.

【White House Weighs Mythos AI Access for Federal Agencies】The White House is preparing to give select federal agencies access to a version of Anthropic’s Mythos AI, with safeguards under development and more details expected in coming weeks. The model, currently limited to a small group due to concerns it could enable cyberattacks, has already been briefed to US officials, who are evaluating its potential to both expose and exploit critical software vulnerabilities.
